title: Canastra IV

{{Quote|These slow-moving but very intelligent creatures are the principal form of amphibian life found in the coastal oceans of Canastra IV.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
'''Canastra IV''' was a [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] celestial body with coastal [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] on its surface. It was home to the [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibian]] [[Diet/Legends|herbivore]] known as the [[two-headed tortuce]]. On Canastra IV, several varieties of the creature existed that led different lifestyles. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the two-headed tortuce was [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] as a gourmet [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] source due to being seen as a delicacy throughout the Core Worlds.

==Description==
{{Quote|Some are deep-diving sea dwellers that live off underwater plant life. Others are land-dwellers who return to the sea when local roots and other edible plants in a given region are exhausted.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
Canastra IV was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="Creatures" /> situated in the [[Canastra system/Legends|Canastra system]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" /> It featured coastal [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]], with the local [[Plant/Legends|flora]] including both land and [[Water/Legends|underwater]] plants. The Canastran coastal oceans were inhabited by [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibian]] creatures, the principal form of which was the [[two-headed tortuce]]—a relatively unknown [[Diet/Legends|herbivorous]] creature with no known natural predators.<ref name="Creatures" />

Several types of two-headed tortuces existed on Canastra IV, including deep-diving sea dwellers that fed on underwater plants as well as land dwellers, who, after exhausting a given region's supply of roots and other edible plants, returned to the sea. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the two-headed tortuce was one of the few multi-[[Brain/Legends|brained]] creatures to survive in the Core Worlds.<ref name="Creatures" />

==History==
{{Quote|This inoffensive creature is seen as a culinary delicacy throughout the Core. As such, thousands are harvested as a gourmet food source each year.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
By the time of the Galactic Empire's existence, Canastra IV's two-headed tortuces were seen as a [[Foodstuff/Legends|culinary]] delicacy throughout the Core Worlds, and as a result, each [[Local year|year]] thousands of the creatures were [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] down and exported throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], with efforts to ban such practices being ineffective. The tortuces' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was mentioned in an entry of a [[Holo-brochure|holobrochure]] for the [[Tagge Holographic Museum]] of<ref name="Creatures" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE Chandrila">{{CSWECite|I|119|Chandrila}}</ref> [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] dedicated to the creature.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Behind the scenes==
Canastra IV was mentioned in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The section of the book mentioning the celestial body was authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]], with Canastra IV being depicted in an illustration by [[Dan Schaefer]].<ref name="Creatures" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Canastra [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Canastra IV, in grid square K-10.<ref name="TEA" />
